name: Nominal sentences and the zero copula
description: Make simple identity and classification statements; Burmese normally omits an overt present-tense copula.

example.text: သူက ဆရာပါ။

name: Topic marker က
description: Mark the person or thing under discussion with က in neutral spoken clauses.

example.text: ကျွန်မက မေပါ။

name: Object marker ကို
description: Mark a definite or specific direct object with ကို.

example.text: ကျွန်တော် ထမင်းကို စားတယ်။

name: Location marker မှာ
description: Use မှာ for the place where an action or state occurs in everyday speech.

example.text: သူ အိမ်မှာ ရှိတယ်။

name: Destination with ကို
description: Use ကို after a destination with common motion verbs.

example.text: မနက်ဖြန် ကျောင်းကို သွားမယ်။
example.translation: I will go to school tomorrow.

name: Realis sentence ending တယ်
description: End ordinary affirmative present and past statements with တယ် in conversational Burmese.

example.text: နေ့တိုင်း မြန်မာစာ လေ့လာတယ်။

name: Future and intention with မယ်
description: Use မယ် for future events, plans, and speaker intention.

example.text: ဒီည စာဖတ်မယ်။

name: Verb negation with မ…ဘူး
description: Negate a verb by placing မ before it and ဘူး at the end of the clause.

example.text: ကျွန်မ ကော်ဖီ မသောက်ဘူး။

name: Yes/no questions with လား
description: Turn a statement into an everyday yes/no question with clause-final လား.

example.text: ခင်ဗျား မြန်မာစကား ပြောတတ်လား။

name: Content questions with လဲ
description: Use a question word and clause-final လဲ for open information questions.

example.text: ဒါ ဘာလဲ။

name: Politeness with ပါ
description: Add ပါ to make statements, requests, and answers polite.

name: Possession with ရဲ့
description: Link a possessor to a following noun with ရဲ့ in conversational Burmese.

example.text: ဒါ ကျွန်မရဲ့ စာအုပ်ပါ။

name: Contextual plural with တွေ
description: Add တွေ when plurality is relevant; leave nouns unmarked when context already supplies number.

example.text: ကလေးတွေ ပန်းခြံမှာ ကစားနေတယ်။
example.translation: The children are playing in the park.

name: Progressive aspect with နေ
description: Place နေ after a verb to show an action or state continuing at the reference time.

example.text: အပြင်မှာ မိုးရွာနေတယ်။

name: Past remoteness with ခဲ့
description: Use ခဲ့ to locate an event away from the present, often as a definite past experience or action.

example.text: မနှစ်က ပုဂံကို သွားခဲ့တယ်။
example.translation: I went to Bagan last year.

name: Completion and changed state with ပြီ
description: Use ပြီ when an event is complete or a new state now holds.

name: Sequential actions with ပြီး
description: Link a completed action to the next action with ပြီး.

example.translation: After showering, I go to work.

name: Learned ability with တတ်
description: Use တတ် for an acquired ability or something that characteristically happens.

example.text: သူ စက်ဘီး စီးတတ်တယ်။

name: Desire with ချင်
description: Place ချင် after a verb to say that the subject wants to perform it.

example.text: မြန်မာနိုင်ငံကို သွားချင်တယ်။
example.translation: I want to go to Myanmar.

name: Permission and possibility with ရ
description: Use ရ after a verb for permission or situational possibility, with context distinguishing the sense.

example.text: ဒီမှာ ဓာတ်ပုံ ရိုက်လို့ရတယ်။

name: Reason with လို့ and ကြောင့်
description: Give a reason with conversational လို့ or noun-like ကြောင့်, choosing the form to fit the clause.

example.translation: I stay home because it is raining.

name: Comparison with ထက် and ပို
description: Mark the comparison standard with ထက် and optionally emphasize the greater degree with ပို.

example.text: ဒီနေ့ မနေ့ကထက် ပိုပူတယ်။

name: Relative clauses with တဲ့
description: Put a clause ending in တဲ့ before a noun to identify or describe it in conversational Burmese.

example.text: မနေ့က ဝယ်တဲ့ စာအုပ်က ကောင်းတယ်။
example.translation: The book I bought yesterday is good.

name: Numerals and classifiers
description: Count people and things with the appropriate classifier, normally placing the numeral-classifier phrase after the noun.

example.text: လက်ဖက်ရည် နှစ်ခွက် ပေးပါ။
example.translation: Please give me two cups of tea.

name: Real conditions with ရင်
description: Use ရင် after a clause for likely, open, and habitual if/when conditions.

example.text: အချိန်ရရင် ပြတိုက်ကို သွားမယ်။
example.translation: If I have time, I will go to the museum.

name: Concession with ပေမယ့်
description: Link contrasting clauses with ပေမယ့် to express although or but.

example.translation: Although it is raining, they play football.

name: Reported speech with လို့
description: Mark quoted thoughts and speech with လို့ before verbs such as say, think, ask, or know.

example.text: သူ မနက်ဖြန် လာမယ်လို့ ပြောတယ်။
example.translation: She said that she would come tomorrow.

name: Clause nominalization with တာ
description: Turn a conversational clause into a noun-like unit with တာ so it can be a topic, object, or complement.

example.text: စာဖတ်တာ ကျွန်မ ဝါသနာပါတယ်။

name: Nominalization with ခြင်း
description: Use ခြင်း for formal or written nominalization of actions and states.

example.text: နေ့စဉ် လေ့ကျင့်ခြင်းက အရေးကြီးတယ်။

name: Adversative passive with ခံရ
description: Express undergoing an unwanted or externally caused event with a verb followed by ခံရ.

example.text: သူ့စက်ဘီး ခိုးခံရတယ်။

name: Benefactive actions with ပေး
description: Place ပေး after an action done for someone else's benefit or as a service.

example.text: ဒီစာကို ဘာသာပြန်ပေးပါ။
example.translation: Please translate this letter for me.

name: Trying an action with ကြည့်
description: Use ကြည့် after a verb to mean trying it and seeing the result.

example.text: ဒီဟင်းကို စားကြည့်ပါ။

name: Scalar focus with တောင် and မှ
description: Use focus particles such as တောင် and မှ to express even, only then, or a strong scalar contrast.

example.text: သူ့နာမည်တောင် ကျွန်တော် မသိဘူး။
example.translation: I do not even know his name.

name: Spoken and written sentence endings
description: Recognize common conversational endings such as တယ် and တဲ့ alongside formal written သည် and သော.

example.text: ဤမြို့သည် မြစ်ကမ်းဘေးတွင် တည်ရှိသည်။
example.translation: This city is situated beside the river.

name: Conversation-managing particles
description: Use particles such as နော်, ပေါ့, လေ, and တော့ to manage shared knowledge, insistence, softening, and topic shifts.

example.text: မနက်ဖြန် စောစောလာနော်။

name: Same-subject sequencing with ကာ
description: Use written-style ကာ to link closely related actions that share a subject.

example.text: သူ ပြုံးကာ အခန်းထဲ ဝင်လာသည်။
